claudeMd:
# Task: Add Obstacles, Coins, and Score Tracking to Teeter You are enhancing a browser-based head-tilt ball game by adding wall obstacles, collectible coins, and a score HUD. ## Codebase Pure static HTML+JS served by nginx. No npm, no build step. - `index.html` — Main page with Three.js importmap and overlay UI - `js/main.js` — Game loop, state management, wires tracker to physics to renderer - `js/tracker.js` — MediaPipe FaceLandmarker head tilt detection (DO NOT MODIFY) - `js/physics.js` — Ball physics (lateral movement, forward speed, falling) - `js/renderer.js` — Three.js scene (track, ball, camera, lighting) - `Dockerfile` + `nginx.conf` — Docker build serving on port 8080 ## Libraries - Three.js v0.183.2 (CDN via importmap): all 3D rendering - No new dependencies allowed ## What to Implement ### 1. Widen Track (renderer.js) - Change `TRACK_WIDTH` from 3 to 4.5 - Edge lines auto-update since they use `TRACK_WIDTH / 2` - Physics gets the new width via `getTrackConfig()` — no physics change needed for this ### 2. Add Obstacles (renderer.js + physics.js) **Renderer — obstacle creation:** - Generate obstacle positions deterministically using a simple seeded PRNG (seed = 42) - Place obstacles starting from Z = `BALL_START_Z + 5` (safe zone), then every 7-9 Z units - Each obstacle: `BoxGeometry(1.5, 1.0, 0.4)` with `MeshStandardMaterial({ color: 0xcc2222, roughness: 0.5, metalness: 0.2 })` - Lateral position: random within `[-TRACK_WIDTH/2 + 0.75, TRACK_WIDTH/2 - 0.75]` — the obstacle stays fully on the track - Position Y: `TRACK_HEIGHT/2 + 0.5` (sitting on track surface) - Add each obstacle mesh to the scene, store in an array - Export `getObstacles()` returning array of `{ x, z, halfWidth: 0.75, halfDepth: 0.2 }` for physics **Physics — obstacle collision:** - In `initPhysics(config)`, store `config.obstacles` array - In `updateOnTrack()`, after position update, check each obstacle: ```js // AABB collision: ball center vs obstacle bounds (expanded by ball radius) if (Math.abs(ball.x - obs.x) < obs.halfWidth + trackConfig.ballRadius && Math.abs(ball.z - obs.z) < obs.halfDepth + trackConfig.ballRadius) { ball.falling = true; return { ...result, obstacleHit: true }; } ``` - When `obstacleHit` is true, main.js triggers the same falling/reset flow as falling off edge ### 3. Add Coins (renderer.js + physics.js + main.js) **Renderer — coin creation:** - Generate coin positions between obstacles: 2-3 coins between each pair of obstacles - Each coin: `TorusGeometry(0.25, 0.08, 8, 16)` with `MeshStandardMaterial({ color: 0xffd700, metalness: 0.8, roughness: 0.2 })` - Rotate initial X by `Math.PI / 2` so the torus faces the player (flat side forward) - Position Y: `TRACK_HEIGHT/2 + 0.5` (floating slightly above track) - Lateral position: random within `[-TRACK_WIDTH/2 + 0.5, TRACK_WIDTH/2 - 0.5]` - Store coin meshes in array, export `getCoins()` returning `{ x, z }` array - Export `hideCoin(index)` — sets `coinMeshes[index].visible = false` - Export `showAllCoins()` — sets all coins visible again - Export `updateCoinRotation(dt)` — rotates all visible coins on Y-axis: `mesh.rotation.y += 2.0 * dt` **Physics — coin collection:** - In `initPhysics(config)`, store `config.coins` array and a `coinsCollected` boolean array (same length, all false) - In `updateOnTrack()`, check each uncollected coin: ```js const dx = ball.x - coin.x; const dz = ball.z - coin.z; if (dx * dx + dz * dz < 0.8 * 0.8) { // collection radius coinsCollected[i] = true; collectedThisFrame.push(i); } ``` - Return `coinsCollected: collectedThisFrame` in the result object (empty array if none) - In `resetBall()`, also reset `coinsCollected` array to all false **Main.js — coin handling:** - After `updatePhysics()`, check `result.coinsCollected` — for each index, call `hideCoin(index)` and increment score - In reset handler, call `showAllCoins()` and set score to 0 ### 4. Score HUD (index.html + main.js) **HTML:** - Add a `
Score: 0
` element in the body - CSS: `position: fixed; top: 20px; left: 20px; color: #fff; font-family: same as overlay; font-size: 1.8em; font-weight: 700; text-shadow: 2px 2px 4px rgba(0,0,0,0.5); z-index: 10; pointer-events: none;` - Initially hidden (display: none), shown when game starts (state = playing) **Main.js:** - Get reference: `const scoreEl = document.getElementById('score');` - Maintain `let score = 0;` - On coin collection: `score += 1; scoreEl.textContent = 'Score: ' + score;` - On reset: `score = 0; scoreEl.textContent = 'Score: 0';` - Show score element when game starts: `scoreEl.style.display = 'block';` ### 5. Handle Track Wrapping - When ball wraps (Z > halfLength), call `showAllCoins()` from main.js and reset physics coin tracking - Physics should return `wrapped: true` when wrapping occurs so main.js can react - Score keeps accumulating through wraps (only resets on death) ## Conventions - No npm/node — pure static JS with ES module imports - CDN-only: Three.js v0.183.2 - Code style: 2-space indent, single quotes, const/let (no var) - Match existing naming patterns (camelCase functions, UPPER_CASE constants) - Keep changes minimal and focused ## Gotchas - `TRACK_WIDTH` is used for track geometry AND edge line positions — changing it once updates both - Physics boundary check uses `trackConfig.trackWidth / 2` from `getTrackConfig()` — automatically picks up new width - The seeded PRNG for obstacle/coin placement ensures consistent layouts across reloads - Obstacle collision must be checked BEFORE track-edge boundary check in `updateOnTrack()` to avoid the ball falling off the edge and through an obstacle simultaneously - Coin collection check should happen BEFORE obstacle check — collect the coin even if also hitting an obstacle on the same frame - `ballMesh.castShadow = true` is already set; new obstacle/coin meshes should also cast shadows for visual consistency - Track wrapping logic in physics.js (line 61-63) needs to return a `wrapped` flag ## Testing - `docker build -t teeter .` must succeed - Visual verification: track is wider, red wall obstacles visible on track, golden rotating coins visible, score HUD in top-left corner - Gameplay: ball hitting obstacle triggers fall/reset, ball passing through coin makes it disappear and score increments, score resets on death

checks: The track is noticeably wider than before (approximately 4-5 units wide), giving the ball more room to maneuver laterally
Red/dark-colored wall obstacles are visible sitting on the track surface at various lateral positions along the track length
Obstacles are NOT placed in the first few units near the ball's starting position — there is a clear safe zone at the start
Golden coin-shaped objects (torus/ring shapes) are visible floating slightly above the track between the obstacles
The coins are slowly rotating on their vertical axis, creating a spinning animation
A score display reading 'Score: 0' is visible in the top-left corner of the screen during gameplay
When the ball rolls into a wall obstacle, it triggers the same falling/reset behavior as falling off the edge
When the ball passes close to a coin, the coin disappears and the score number increments by 1
After the ball resets (from falling off edge or hitting an obstacle), the score resets back to 0
After reset, all previously collected coins reappear on the track
The ball still moves laterally in response to head tilt, and the track remains static and flat
Performance remains smooth with no visible frame drops or stuttering from the added obstacles and coins
